headlineSummary: HN sentiment is mostly neutral overall, with Google Gemini and OpenAI showing small positive signals in a close tie, while Anthropic is pulled between praise and a sharp criticism in limited evidence [E1][E2].

ranking.summary: Anthropic looks mixed neutral: one comment credits Claude with the broader AI-assisted coding wave, but another criticizes Claude Code sharply, so the result rests on limited evidence [E1][E2].
Google Gemini has a small positive tilt from a comment saying it helped make weekend-scale app building feasible, and it is in a close tie with OpenAI [E1].
OpenAI also leans slightly positive from the same comment praising Codex for lowering the barrier to building useful cross-platform apps, and it is in a close tie with Google Gemini [E1].

evidence.excerpt: I'm seeing that "great-ai-unlock" is happening. I see in last month a lot of new software being codeveloped with claude/codex/gemini/you-name it. Before, it was too costly to do sth like the Posture app: here, you would have to know Swift and apple apis to write such tool. Would you be C# (very good) programmer with free weekend, and an idea: no cookie for ya. These days, due to "great-ai-unlock" your skills can be easily transferred and used to cross platforms boundary and code such useful app in a weekend or so.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude and credits it as part of a broader wave enabling useful software to be built much more easily.
The comment explicitly names Codex and presents it as helping lower the barrier to building apps quickly across platforms.
The comment explicitly names Gemini and includes it in praise for tools that make weekend-scale app creation feasible.

evidence.excerpt: Thank god it’s not another React, Solid, Typescript, what have you, web abomination inside the terminal (claude code, opencode, I’m looking at you).

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names Claude Code and calls it a "web abomination inside the terminal," which is strong criticism.
